title: AcyclicIffTopologicalOrder
lean_code_with_comments:
theorem acyclic_iff_topological_order (G : CDMG Node) :
    G.IsAcyclic ↔ ∃ lt : Node → Node → Prop, G.IsTopologicalOrder lt
-- claim_3_2 -- end statement
:= by
  -- TeX proof: tex/claim_3_2_proof_AcyclicIffTopologicalOrder.tex
  constructor
  · -- (⇒) Acyclic ⇒ a topological order exists.
    --
    -- Following the LN proof: define the reflexive walk-reachability
    -- relation `le₀ u v := u = v ∨ ∃ directed walk u → v of length ≥ 1`,
    -- show it is a partial order (refl/trans via walk concatenation,
    -- antisymm via acyclicity), then apply Mathlib's Szpilrajn
    -- extension (`extend_partialOrder`) to obtain a linear order `s`
    -- extending `le₀`.  Reading `lt u v := s u v ∧ u ≠ v` gives the
    -- strict total order witnessing `IsTopologicalOrder`.
    --
    -- This is a "semantic" Leanification of the LN's inductive
    -- parent-less-pick construction: where the LN picks `v_1, …, v_K`
    -- by hand and reads off the order, we package the same partial
    -- order (walk reachability) and invoke Szpilrajn to do the
    -- enumeration.  The two constructions produce the same kind of
    -- witness (any topological order qualifies; the LN never picks a
    -- canonical one).
    intro hac
    let le₀ : Node → Node → Prop := fun u v =>
      u = v ∨ ∃ p : Walk G u v, p.IsDirectedWalk ∧ p.length ≥ 1
    -- Reflexivity, transitivity, antisymmetry of `le₀`.
    have hrefl : ∀ a, le₀ a a := fun _ => Or.inl rfl
    have htrans : ∀ a b c, le₀ a b → le₀ b c → le₀ a c := by
      intros a b c hab hbc
      rcases hab with heq | ⟨p, hp_dir, hp_len⟩
      · subst heq; exact hbc
      rcases hbc with heq | ⟨q, hq_dir, hq_len⟩
      · subst heq; exact Or.inr ⟨p, hp_dir, hp_len⟩
      · refine Or.inr ⟨p.comp q,
          Walk.isDirectedWalk_comp p q hp_dir hq_dir, ?_⟩
        rw [Walk.length_comp]; omega
    have hantisymm : ∀ a b, le₀ a b → le₀ b a → a = b := by
      intros a b hab hba
      rcases hab with heq | ⟨p, hp_dir, hp_len⟩
      · exact heq
      rcases hba with heq | ⟨q, hq_dir, hq_len⟩
      · exact heq.symm
      · -- Both `p : a → b` and `q : b → a` non-trivial directed walks.
        -- Concatenate to a non-trivial directed walk `a → a`, contradict
        -- acyclicity at `a` (`a ∈ G` from the source of `p`).
        exfalso
        have ha : a ∈ G :=
          Walk.source_in_G_of_directedWalk_pos p hp_dir hp_len
        refine hac a ha ⟨p.comp q,
          Walk.isDirectedWalk_comp p q hp_dir hq_dir, ?_⟩
        rw [Walk.length_comp]; omega
    haveI : Std.Refl le₀ := ⟨hrefl⟩
    haveI : IsTrans Node le₀ := ⟨htrans⟩
    haveI : Std.Antisymm le₀ := ⟨hantisymm⟩
    haveI : IsPreorder Node le₀ := {}
    haveI : IsPartialOrder Node le₀ := {}
    -- Szpilrajn extension: a linear order `s` with `le₀ ≤ s`.
    obtain ⟨s, hs_lo, h_sub⟩ := extend_partialOrder le₀
    -- Promote `hs_lo` to an active typeclass instance so the inherited
    -- `IsTrans` / `Std.Total` / `Std.Antisymm` are resolvable by name
    -- (without `haveI` they sit as a regular hypothesis, invisible to
    -- typeclass synthesis — hence the unification failures otherwise
    -- triggered by `Std.Total.total v w`).
    haveI : IsLinearOrder Node s := hs_lo
    -- The strict version of `s` is our topological order.
    refine ⟨fun u v => s u v ∧ u ≠ v, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
    · -- Irreflexivity on `G`: `¬ (s v v ∧ v ≠ v)` from `v ≠ v`.
      intro v _ hlt
      exact hlt.2 rfl
    · -- Transitivity on `G`.
      intros u _ v _ w _ huv hvw
      obtain ⟨hsuv, hne_uv⟩ := huv
      obtain ⟨hsvw, hne_vw⟩ := hvw
      refine ⟨IsTrans.trans u v w hsuv hsvw, ?_⟩
      intro huw
      subst huw
      -- `s u v` and `s v u` with antisymm gives `u = v`, contradicting `hne_uv`.
      exact hne_uv (Std.Antisymm.antisymm u v hsuv hsvw)
    · -- Trichotomy on `G`: from totality of `s`.
      intros v _ w _
      by_cases h : v = w
      · right; left; exact h
      · rcases (Std.Total.total (r := s) v w) with hvw | hwv
        · left; exact ⟨hvw, h⟩
        · right; right; exact ⟨hwv, fun heq => h heq.symm⟩
    · -- Parent precedes: `u ∈ Pa^G(w) → lt u w`.
      intros u w hu_in_Pa
      obtain ⟨hu, huw_E⟩ := hu_in_Pa
      have hw : w ∈ G :=
        Finset.mem_union_right _ (G.hE_subset huw_E).2
      have hle₀_uw : le₀ u w :=
        Or.inr (Walk.singleEdge_directedWalk hw huw_E)
      have hsuw : s u w := h_sub _ _ hle₀_uw
      refine ⟨hsuw, ?_⟩
      intro heq
      subst heq
      -- A self-loop `(u, u) ∈ G.E` would give a length-1 directed walk
      -- `u → u`, contradicting acyclicity at `u`.
      exact hac u hu (Walk.singleEdge_directedWalk hu huw_E)
  · -- (⇐) A topological order ⇒ acyclic.
    --
    -- LN argument: a non-trivial directed walk `v = v_0 → v_1 → ⋯ →
    -- v_n = v` with `n ≥ 1` would give a chain `v_0 < v_1 < ⋯ < v_n =
    -- v_0` under any topological order, contradicting irreflexivity.
    -- Encoded as `Walk.lt_of_directedWalk_pos` above (which uses
    -- parent-precedes and transitivity to walk along edges) and
    -- combined with the topological order's irreflexivity field.
    rintro ⟨lt, hi, htr, htri, hp⟩
    intro v hv ⟨p, hp_dir, hp_len⟩
    exact hi v hv (Walk.lt_of_directedWalk_pos htr hp p hp_dir hp_len)
lean_code_without_comments:
theorem acyclic_iff_topological_order (G : CDMG Node) :
    G.IsAcyclic ↔ ∃ lt : Node → Node → Prop, G.IsTopologicalOrder lt

:= by

  constructor
  ·

    intro hac
    let le₀ : Node → Node → Prop := fun u v =>
      u = v ∨ ∃ p : Walk G u v, p.IsDirectedWalk ∧ p.length ≥ 1

    have hrefl : ∀ a, le₀ a a := fun _ => Or.inl rfl
    have htrans : ∀ a b c, le₀ a b → le₀ b c → le₀ a c := by
      intros a b c hab hbc
      rcases hab with heq | ⟨p, hp_dir, hp_len⟩
      · subst heq; exact hbc
      rcases hbc with heq | ⟨q, hq_dir, hq_len⟩
      · subst heq; exact Or.inr ⟨p, hp_dir, hp_len⟩
      · refine Or.inr ⟨p.comp q,
          Walk.isDirectedWalk_comp p q hp_dir hq_dir, ?_⟩
        rw [Walk.length_comp]; omega
    have hantisymm : ∀ a b, le₀ a b → le₀ b a → a = b := by
      intros a b hab hba
      rcases hab with heq | ⟨p, hp_dir, hp_len⟩
      · exact heq
      rcases hba with heq | ⟨q, hq_dir, hq_len⟩
      · exact heq.symm
      ·

        exfalso
        have ha : a ∈ G :=
          Walk.source_in_G_of_directedWalk_pos p hp_dir hp_len
        refine hac a ha ⟨p.comp q,
          Walk.isDirectedWalk_comp p q hp_dir hq_dir, ?_⟩
        rw [Walk.length_comp]; omega
    haveI : Std.Refl le₀ := ⟨hrefl⟩
    haveI : IsTrans Node le₀ := ⟨htrans⟩
    haveI : Std.Antisymm le₀ := ⟨hantisymm⟩
    haveI : IsPreorder Node le₀ := {}
    haveI : IsPartialOrder Node le₀ := {}

    obtain ⟨s, hs_lo, h_sub⟩ := extend_partialOrder le₀

    haveI : IsLinearOrder Node s := hs_lo

    refine ⟨fun u v => s u v ∧ u ≠ v, ?_, ?_, ?_, ?_⟩
    ·
      intro v _ hlt
      exact hlt.2 rfl
    ·
      intros u _ v _ w _ huv hvw
      obtain ⟨hsuv, hne_uv⟩ := huv
      obtain ⟨hsvw, hne_vw⟩ := hvw
      refine ⟨IsTrans.trans u v w hsuv hsvw, ?_⟩
      intro huw
      subst huw

      exact hne_uv (Std.Antisymm.antisymm u v hsuv hsvw)
    ·
      intros v _ w _
      by_cases h : v = w
      · right; left; exact h
      · rcases (Std.Total.total (r := s) v w) with hvw | hwv
        · left; exact ⟨hvw, h⟩
        · right; right; exact ⟨hwv, fun heq => h heq.symm⟩
    ·
      intros u w hu_in_Pa
      obtain ⟨hu, huw_E⟩ := hu_in_Pa
      have hw : w ∈ G :=
        Finset.mem_union_right _ (G.hE_subset huw_E).2
      have hle₀_uw : le₀ u w :=
        Or.inr (Walk.singleEdge_directedWalk hw huw_E)
      have hsuw : s u w := h_sub _ _ hle₀_uw
      refine ⟨hsuw, ?_⟩
      intro heq
      subst heq

      exact hac u hu (Walk.singleEdge_directedWalk hu huw_E)
  ·

    rintro ⟨lt, hi, htr, htri, hp⟩
    intro v hv ⟨p, hp_dir, hp_len⟩
    exact hi v hv (Walk.lt_of_directedWalk_pos htr hp p hp_dir hp_len)
lean_explanation:
The theorem signature binds a single graph `G : CDMG Node`, where the implicit `Node : Type*` is the ambient vertex carrier — the silent universe of vertex labels that the LN's $J \cup V$ is drawn from — and `[DecidableEq Node]` is inherited from the surrounding `variable` block. The decidability instance is load-bearing: it is what `G.IsAcyclic`'s recursive `Walk.IsDirectedWalk` check from `def_3_6` and `G.IsTopologicalOrder lt`'s bounded membership quantifiers (`v ∈ G`, which unfolds to `v ∈ G.J ∪ G.V` via the `Membership` instance from `def_3_2`) reach through to typecheck. Stronger structure on `Node` (no `Fintype`, no `LinearOrder`) is deliberately *not* requested at this layer.

The biconditional reads `G.IsAcyclic ↔ ∃ lt : Node → Node → Prop, G.IsTopologicalOrder lt`. The left-hand side is the predicate from `def_3_6`: no non-trivial directed walk from any node of `G` returns to itself. The right-hand side existentially quantifies over a *bare* binary relation `lt : Node → Node → Prop` — not a Mathlib `LinearOrder` instance, not a subtype-restricted strict order — and asserts that `G.IsTopologicalOrder lt` holds. The latter is the four-conjunct predicate from `def_3_8` packaging irreflexivity, transitivity, trichotomy (each restricted to `G.J ∪ G.V` via the bounded `∀ v ∈ G` form), and the parent-precedes-child clause `v ∈ G.Pa w → lt v w`.

A few Lean / Mathlib idioms worth flagging. The relation `lt : Node → Node → Prop` is the standard idiom for an external strict order — a curried `Prop`-valued binary predicate — and is what `def_3_8`'s "Downstream consumers" design note anticipated this row would quantify over. The notation `v ∈ G` is sugar from the `Membership Node (CDMG Node)` instance, so the LN's literal "for every $v, w \in J \cup V$" transports verbatim onto the Lean text. The existential ranges over *all* relations on the full `Node` type; the four conjuncts bound `lt`'s behaviour to the carrier `G.J ∪ G.V`, leaving nodes outside the graph unrelated.

The shape of the encoding is intentionally minimal: hypothesis `G : CDMG Node`; conclusion an `Iff` between a `Prop`-valued predicate on `G` and an existential over a `Prop`-valued binary relation. Consumers reach `.mp` (acyclic ⟹ a topological order exists) or `.mpr` (topological order ⟹ acyclic) and, on `.mp`, destructure with `obtain ⟨lt, hi, htr, htri, hp⟩ := …` to recover the relation and its four conjuncts in the order `def_3_8` lists them.
design_choices:
**Biconditional `↔` mirrors the LN's "if and only if" verbatim.** A single `Iff` packages both directions: `.mp` (acyclic ⟹ a topological order exists) is the workhorse projection for downstream causality results that thread a parent-first traversal over an ADMG, while `.mpr` (topological order ⟹ acyclic) is the certificate route for causal-discovery algorithms that construct an ordering and need to conclude acyclicity. Splitting into two named theorems was considered and rejected: the LN treats this as one claim, every downstream site needs only one direction at a time and reaches it via `.mp` / `.mpr` at zero cost, and the bundled `↔` keeps the LN ↔ Lean cross-reference grep-able under a single name.

**The right-hand side existential binds a bare `Node → Node → Prop`, not a bundled order structure.** Three bundled alternatives were considered and rejected. `∃ lt : LinearOrder Node, …` forces totality, transitivity, and antisymmetry on the *full* `Node` type, excluding perfectly valid orders that leave nodes outside $J \cup V$ unrelated — yet the LN only requires totality on $J \cup V$. A subtype-restricted `∃ lt : StrictOrder ↥(G.J ∪ G.V), …` avoids that over-commitment but forces every consumer to coerce nodes across the subtype boundary at every use site; `def_3_8` rejected this on the predicate side for the same reason, and we inherit the choice here. A `∃!`-strengthened or `Classical.choice`-extracted canonical order would inject uniqueness the LN does not commit to: many topological orders generally exist (any linear refinement of the parent-precedence partial order qualifies). Consumers `obtain ⟨lt, hlt⟩ := …` and destructure via `def_3_8`'s four-conjunct `⟨hi, htr, htri, hp⟩` shape.

**Only `(G : CDMG Node)` is hypothesised — no `[Fintype Node]`, no extra typeclasses beyond `[DecidableEq Node]`.** Every shape constraint the biconditional rests on (finite `J, V`, `Disjoint J V`, the edge containment $E \subseteq (J \cup V) \times V$, label symmetry / irreflexivity) is baked into the `CDMG` record from `def_3_1`. `[DecidableEq Node]` is what `G.IsAcyclic`'s walk recursion and `G.IsTopologicalOrder lt`'s membership quantifiers reach through to typecheck. A `[Fintype Node]` binder was considered — the forward proof may want it locally to enumerate `(G.J ∪ G.V).toList` — and rejected at the statement level: the proof body's local needs should not leak into the surface signature, and the `Finset`-valued `G.J`, `G.V` already supply finiteness on the domain the order must be total over.

**Labels `L` are correctly absent from both sides.** Acyclicity inspects only directed walks built from `G.E`; a topological order references only the parent set `G.Pa w`, a directed-edge construct from `def_3_5`. Neither side reads `G.L`. Acyclicity and the existence of a topological order are properties of the $(J, V, E)$-skeleton of `G` alone, matching the LN's silence on labels here.

**Non-canonical witness — intentional, not an API gap.** The `∃` deliberately does not designate a canonical topological order: many may exist, and the LN never picks one. Downstream theorems that need to fix a specific order must either pass `lt` as an explicit hypothesis or `Classical.choice`-extract it locally at the use site (inheriting the non-canonicality).
